other_observers: Cole Wild, Allan Burns, and others I can't remember; don't know
description: the bird was a small flycatcher, notcieably smaller than nearby Mountain Bluebirds. The overall shape was Phoebe like, but more stocky, mostly due to a shorter tail. The bird had a slight crest. The bill was very small, short and thin, and black. The legs and eye were black.  The bird's head was bright crimson red, except for a very dark gray eye line that got increasingly wider behind the eye and connected with a nape and upperparts the same color. The underparts were entirely crimson red, though there was a small patch on the chest that seemed to be a bit paler. The tail was slightly forked, and the same dark gray/black color as the upperparts.Behaviors: the bird was flycatching from the tops of mullens out in a field, near a large flock of Mountain Bluebirds.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
